publication.changeSummary: Added digest-pinned deterministic executable evidence for Enumerations; AI-assisted draft remains human-unreviewed and exam-ineligible.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify language-version accuracy, distractor validity, equivalence, and grading evidence before formal use.
items.title: Enumerations: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? A C enum is a runtime-checked closed set that can never hold a value outside its listed enumerators.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Use enums for named integral states but validate external values explicitly; the type alone is not a universal runtime membership check.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Use enums for named integral states but validate external values explicitly; the type alone is not a universal runtime membership check.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Use enums for named integral states but validate external values explicitly; the type alone is not a universal runtime membership check.
A C enum is a runtime-checked closed set that can never hold a value outside its listed enumerators.

answer.explanation: Use enums for named integral states but validate external values explicitly; the type alone is not a universal runtime membership check.

items.title: Enumerations: executable application
items.prompt: Read an external integer state, convert only values 0 through 2 to a named enum, and print idle, running, stopped, or invalid with exhaustive control flow.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: closed named states with explicit external-value validation

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises closed named states with explicit external-value validation under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
